title: dryopteris sieboldii var. toyamae
intro: Dryopteris sieboldii var. toyamae, also known as the Toyama shield fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the Dryopteris genus and is native to Japan. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Toyama shield fern include its ability to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and promote wound healing. Toyama shield fern is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, which is used to treat various health conditions, or as a dried powder, which is added to herbal teas and capsules for its medicinal properties.
